description:
Represents a serverless worker that executes code in response to triggers or direct invocations.
Workers are the primary compute resource in serverless applications, designed to be stateless and ephemeral.

code.description: Code for the worker, either a pre-built image or source code to be built.

src.description: The source directory to build from

binaryName.description: Name of the binary to build and run

oneOf.description: TypeScript/JavaScript compiled to single executable with Bun

binaryName.description: Name of the compiled binary (defaults to package.json name if not specified)

commandsEnabled.description:
Whether the worker can receive remote commands via the Commands protocol.
When enabled, the platform pushes commands into the Worker runtime,
which executes registered handlers.

concurrencyLimit.description:
Maximum number of concurrent executions allowed for the worker.
None means platform default applies.

environment.description: Key-value pairs to set as environment variables for the worker.

id.description:
Identifier for the worker. Must contain only alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores ([A-Za-z0-9-_]).
Maximum 64 characters.

items.description: Reference to a resource by its stable id and resource type.

type.description: Resource type identifier that determines the specific kind of resource. This field is used for polymorphic deserialization and resource-specific behavior.

links.description: List of resource references this worker depends on.

memoryMb.description:
Memory allocated to the worker in megabytes (MB).
Default: 512

Platform-specific constraints:
- **AWS Lambda**: 128–10240 MB in 1 MB increments
- **GCP Cloud Run**: 128–32768 MB
- **Azure Container Apps**: fixed CPU/memory pairs — 512, 1024, 1536, 2048, 2560,
  3072, 3584, 4096 MB. Values below 512 are automatically rounded up at deploy time.

permissions.description:
Permission profile name that defines the permissions granted to this worker.
This references a profile defined in the stack's permission definitions.

items.description: Public endpoint configuration for Worker resources.

hostLabel.description: Optional DNS label override for generated endpoint hostnames.

wildcardSubdomains.description: Whether to route wildcard subdomains to this endpoint.

publicEndpoints.description: Public endpoints exposed by this worker.

oneOf.description:
Optional readiness probe configuration.
Only applicable for workers with Public ingress.
When configured, the probe will be executed after provisioning/update to verify the worker is ready.

method.description:
HTTP method to use for the probe request.
Default: GET

path.description:
Path to request for the probe (e.g., "/health", "/ready").
Default: "/"

timeoutSeconds.description:
Maximum execution time for the worker in seconds.
Constraints: 1‑3600 seconds (platform-specific limits may apply)
Default: 180

oneOf.description: Worker triggered by queue messages (always 1 message per invocation)

type.description: Resource type identifier that determines the specific kind of resource. This field is used for polymorphic deserialization and resource-specific behavior.

oneOf.description: Worker triggered by storage events (object created, deleted, etc.)

type.description: Resource type identifier that determines the specific kind of resource. This field is used for polymorphic deserialization and resource-specific behavior.

oneOf.description: Worker triggered on a schedule (cron expression)

cron.description: Cron expression for scheduling (standard 5-field unix cron)

items.description: Defines what triggers a worker execution.

triggers.description:
List of triggers that define what events automatically invoke this worker.
If empty, the worker is only invokable directly via HTTP calls or platform-specific invocation APIs.
When configured, the worker will be automatically invoked when any of the specified trigger conditions are met.
